source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure that the ‘Log_min_messages’ Flag for a Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance is set at minimum to 'Warning'

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the ‘Log_min_messages’ Flag for a Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance is set at minimum to 'Warning'
input_row.description: The log_min_messages flag defines the minimum message severity level that is considered as an error statement. Messages for error statements are logged with the SQL statement. Valid values include (from lowest to highest severity) DEBUG5, DEBUG4, DEBUG3, DEBUG2, DEBUG1, INFO, NOTICE, WARNING, ERROR, LOG, FATAL, and PANIC. Each severity level includes the subsequent levels mentioned above. ERROR is considered the best practice setting. Changes should only be made in accordance with the organization's logging policy.
input_row.rationale: Auditing helps in troubleshooting operational problems and also permits forensic analysis. If log_min_messages is not set to the correct value, messages may not be classified as error messages appropriately. Setting the threshold to 'Warning' will log messages for the most needed error messages. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances. Impact: Setting the threshold too low will might result in increased log storage size and length, making it difficult to find actual errors. Higher severity levels may cause errors needed to troubleshoot to not be logged. An organization will need to decide their own threshold for logging log_min_messages flag. Note : To effectively turn off logging failing statements, set this parameter to PANIC.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page. 3. Go to the Configuration card. 4. Under Database flags, check the value of log_min_messages flag is set to warning or higher (WARNING|ERROR|LOG|FATAL|PANIC).  From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify that the value of log_min_messages is set to warning or higher . gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_messages")|.value'
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance for which you want to enable the database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add a Database Flag, choose the flag log_min_messages from the drop-down menu and set appropriate value. 6. Click Save to save the changes. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the log_min_messages database flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags log_min_messages=<DEBUG5|DEBUG4|DEBUG3|DEBUG2|DEBUG1|INFO|NOTICE|WARNING|ERRO R|LOG|FATAL|PANIC> Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default log_min_messages is ERROR. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-MESSAGES Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Setting the threshold too low will might result in increased log storage size and length, making it difficult to find actual errors. Higher severity levels may cause errors needed to troubleshoot to not be logged. An organization will need to decide their own threshold for logging log_min_messages flag. Note : To effectively turn off logging failing statements, set this parameter to PANIC.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-MESSAGES Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control to ensure that the `log_min_messages` flag for a Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance is set to 'WARNING' or higher can be verified through GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command as indicated in the audit section. Since this involves checking a specific configuration setting that is exposed via the gcloud command-line tool, it can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_min_messages_threshold_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the command `gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_messages")|.value'` to retrieve the `log_min_messages` flag value for each Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance.
- **Target Resource:** Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances.
- **Configuration Property to Check:** `log_min_messages` database flag.
- **Validation Criteria:** The value should be 'WARNING', 'ERROR', 'LOG', 'FATAL', or 'PANIC'. These levels represent a severity threshold that is appropriate for logging error messages.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** If the `log_min_messages` is set to 'WARNING' or a level higher in severity.
  - **Fail:** If the `log_min_messages` is set to a level lower than 'WARNING' (i.e., DEBUG, INFO, or NOTICE).
  
This automated check leverages the Google Cloud SDK to efficiently ensure compliance with the specified logging threshold across all relevant Cloud SQL instances.
step2_review:
---
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The configuration of the `log_min_messages` flag on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances is directly queryable via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). No manual inspection is required.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:** The `gcloud sql instances describe` command exposes the `settings.databaseFlags` array, which contains the `log_min_messages` flag if set. This can be programmatically checked for all instances.
- **Other GCP Tools:** While GCP Config (now called Config Controller) or Security Command Center do not natively check this flag, the approach using `gcloud` is valid and standard for this type of audit.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:** The command provided is technically correct for extracting the `log_min_messages` flag:
  ```
  gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_messages")|.value'
  ```
  This will return the value if the flag is set.
- **Caveats:**
  - If the flag is not explicitly set, the default value applies (which is 'WARNING' for PostgreSQL, but this should be verified per GCP documentation and not assumed). The script should handle cases where the flag is not set (i.e., `.settings.databaseFlags` does not contain `log_min_messages`).
  - The command assumes `jq` is available, which is common but not universal in all environments.
- **Program Name:** `gcp_sql_instance_log_min_messages_threshold_check` is descriptive but could be more precise. Including "minimum_severity" or "flag" would clarify further.
- **Validation Criteria:** The logic for pass/fail is correct, but the documentation should explicitly list the order of severity and clarify that "higher" means more severe (i.e., numerically lower in PostgreSQL's internal mapping).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Edge Case Handling:**  
  - **Missing Flag:** If `log_min_messages` is not set, the script should document whether to treat this as a pass (default applies) or fail (explicit setting required). CIS benchmarks sometimes require explicit settings.
  - **Multiple Instances:** Recommend providing a loop or bulk query example for all instances in a project, not just a single instance.
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - The same check can be performed using the REST API (`GET https://sqladmin.googleapis.com/sql/v1beta4/projects/{project}/instances/{instance}`) and parsing the response.
  - For automation at scale, consider using the Cloud Asset Inventory API to enumerate instances.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_min_messages_minimum_severity_flag_check`
  - This clarifies the DB engine, resource, flag, and the nature of the check.
- **Documentation:**  
  - Include a reference to the official GCP documentation for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL flags.
  - Clarify the mapping of severity levels and what constitutes "higher" (e.g., WARNING <= value <= PANIC).
- **Output Format:**  
  - Recommend the script outputs the instance name, current value, and pass/fail status for reporting.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP best practices and CIS Benchmark recommendations. The only minor gaps are around edge case handling and naming clarity.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep the automated approach.**
- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Handle cases where the flag is not set (default applies—document how this is treated).
  - Recommend a more descriptive program name.
  - Suggest output/reporting improvements for scalability.
  - Optionally, provide a bulk-check example for all instances in a project.

---

#### **Summary Table**

| Section              | Assessment          | Notes                                                                                     |
|----------------------|--------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE              | Automated check is appropriate and feasible with GCP CLI/API.                             |
| Technical Accuracy   | 8/10               | Command is correct, but needs edge case handling and improved naming.                     |
| Improvements         | See above          | Edge case for unset flag, bulk instance support, naming, documentation.                   |
| Confidence           | HIGH               | Approach is sound and aligns with GCP/CIS best practices.                                 |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY             | Tweak automation, clarify naming, handle unset flag, improve reporting/documentation.     |

---
